title: Blank Canvas Failure
subtitle: Critical absence of visual information creates extreme cognitive load.
problem_statement: The primary business challenge is the complete lack of visual content on the provided screenshots, rendering the platform unusable and creating an insurmountable barrier to user engagement and task completion. Users are presented with a blank white screen, offering no affordance or information scent.

pillars.title: Visibility
pillars.insight: Users experience extreme frustration due to the complete lack of visual feedback or information on the blank screen, leading to a profound sense of being lost and unable to proceed with any task.

pillars.title: Cognitive Load
pillars.insight: The absence of any UI elements, text, or imagery forces users into an infinite cognitive load, as there is no information to process, no actions to take, and no system status to interpret, leading to immediate abandonment.

pillars.title: Speed
pillars.insight: Users cannot perform any actions or complete any tasks, resulting in zero task completion speed and an immediate, unavoidable exit from the platform due to the blank interface.

artifacts.label: Strategy
artifacts.title: Defining the Challenges
questions: How might we ensure *any* content is displayed to users upon arrival?
How might we provide essential visual cues and interactive elements when the screen is entirely blank?
How might we prevent a complete absence of UI elements to guide user interaction?

personas.name: Frustrated Foodie
personas.quote: Where's the food? I see nothing! This website is broken.
personas.bio: Rohan is tech-savvy and relies on online services for convenience. He expects immediate visual feedback and clear pathways to order food. Encountering a blank screen is a critical failure for his fast-paced lifestyle.
needs: To see *any* content on the page
To understand what the website is for
To find a clear entry point for ordering food
frustrations: Seeing a completely blank white page
Uncertainty about whether the site is loading or broken

personas.name: Confused Commuter
personas.quote: Is this broken? I can't even start! There's literally nothing here.
personas.bio: Priya uses her commute time to plan meals and errands. She expects intuitive interfaces that quickly guide her to her goal. A blank screen is not just frustrating; it's a complete roadblock to her productivity.
needs: To have visual elements to click or type into
To receive some form of system feedback
To quickly identify the site's purpose and primary actions
frustrations: No entry point for interaction or navigation
Complete lack of information or instructions
Feeling like the website is unresponsive or non-existent

points.action: Substitute the blank screen with a prominent loading spinner and a 'Loading...' message.
points.benefit: Provides immediate system status feedback, reducing user anxiety and indicating that content is expected.

points.action: Combine a clear error message (e.g., 'Content failed to load') with a 'Retry' button and contact support options.
points.benefit: Informs the user about the problem, offers a path forward, and maintains trust even in failure states.

points.action: Adapt standard website header and footer elements (logo, navigation, legal links) to be present even if primary content fails to load.
points.benefit: Establishes basic branding, navigation, and trust, preventing a completely disorienting blank experience.

points.action: Modify the initial empty state to include illustrative graphics, a clear value proposition, and a primary call to action (e.g., 'Enter your location').
points.benefit: Engages users immediately, communicates purpose, and guides them to the first interaction point, even before full content loads.

pivot.before: A completely blank white screen with no UI elements, text, or content, leading to zero user interaction and immediate abandonment.
pivot.pivot_logic: The fundamental principle of user experience dictates that users need visual cues, content, and interactive elements to engage with a system. A blank screen provides zero affordance, information scent, or system status, resulting in an insurmountable cognitive load and a critical failure to meet basic user expectations.
pivot.after: A functional landing page that immediately displays a clear header (logo, login/signup), a prominent location search input, a compelling promotional banner, and an informative footer, ensuring immediate user engagement and a clear path to task initiation.

stages.actions: User navigates to the URL (e.g., types 'swiggy.com')
stages.friction: Complete lack of visual information; sees only a blank white screen.

stages.actions: User clicks randomly, waits, or tries to scroll, expecting content to appear.
stages.friction: No response from the blank screen; no interactive elements or feedback are present.

stages.actions: User realizes nothing will appear or function, concludes the site is broken.
stages.friction: Inability to perform any action, find information, or understand system status leads to immediate abandonment.

stages.actions: User closes the tab or navigates to a competitor's website.
stages.friction: Total failure to meet user expectations or provide any value.

stages.actions: User remembers the negative experience of a non-functional website.
stages.friction: Brand damage and lost potential customer due to a critical display failure.

annotations.title: Visible Header
annotations.rationale: Provides immediate brand identity and essential navigation points (Login/Signup), ensuring users know where they are and can access account features even on initial load.

annotations.title: Prominent Location Search Input
annotations.rationale: Directs the user to the primary action of finding food by clearly asking for their location, significantly reducing cognitive load and guiding the first interaction.

annotations.title: Informative Footer
annotations.rationale: Offers access to support, legal information, and company details, building trust and providing fallback options for users who might be exploring or need help.
